step_rationale: Anchor for myocardial necrosis; defines MI diagnosis.
mechanism: Cardiac troponin is released from damaged cardiomyocytes upon ischemic necrosis, serving as the diagnostic standard for myocardial infarction.

references.title: Myocardial injury biomarkers and their assays: advances, insights, and future directions.
Type 2 myocardial infarction: a diagnostic and therapeutic challenge in contemporary cardiology.
Circulating miR-379 as a potential novel biomarker for diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction.
references.journal: European review for medical and pharmacological sciences

trials.title: High-sensitivity Cardiac Troponin and CK-MB Concentrations in Patients Undergoing Cardiac Surgery
Reference Interval Validation of High-Sensitivity Cardiac Troponin T Assay in Thai Population
Prospective Multicenter Study for Early Evaluation of Acute Chest Pain
A Randomized Clinical Trial (RCT) of Population and Sex-specific Troponin Cutoffs for Ruling Out Acute Myocardial Infarction - The Danish Study of Population and Sex-Specific Cutoffs of Troponin
interventions: Implementation of the new population and sex-specific 99th percentile URL for cardiac troponin

trials.title: Trial of Switching High-sensitivity Cardiac Troponin Assays in Suspected Acute Coronary Syndrome: an Interrupted Time Series Analysis (TWITCH-ED Study)
High-Sensitivity Cardiac Troponin I as a Diagnostic Marker for AMI Clinical Stage Classification in Acute Myocardial Infarction: A Retrospective Multicenter Study
Prehospital Point-of-Care High-Sensitivity Troponin I for Rule-Out of Acute Myocardial Infarction in Suspected Non-ST-Segment Elevation Acute Coronary Syndrome: A Pilot Study
interventions: Prehospital high-sensitivity cardiac troponin I measurement
